passive.name: Crimson Pact
passive.description: Every 30 points of bonus Health gives Vladimir 1 Ability Power and every 1 point of Ability Power gives Vladimir 1.6 bonus Health (does not stack with itself).

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Innate:}} {{ci|Vladimir}} gains {{sti|ap|{{as|ability power}}}} based on his {{as|'''bonus''' health}}, and {{sti|health|{{as|'''bonus''' health}}}} based on his {{as|ability power}}.
wiki_detail.blurb4: <small>''These bonuses do not stack with each other.''</small>
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Innate:}} '''Vladimir''' gains {{as|({{fd|{{#var:cp1recur}}}}% '''bonus''' health)}} as {{sti|ap|{{as|ability power}}}} and {{as|({{#var:cp2}}% AP)}} as {{sti|health|{{as|'''bonus''' health}}}}. These two bonuses do not stack with each other.
wiki_detail.notes:
* ''Crimson Pact'' only affects '''Vladimir's''' {{as|'''maximum''' health}} and will not increase '''Vladimir's''' {{as|'''current''' health}} to prevent '''Vladimir''' from restoring health whenever his {{as|AP}} fluctuates.
** '''Vladimir's''' {{as|'''current''' health}} can decrease to match his {{as|'''maximum''' health}} if ''Crimson Pact's'' {{as|'''bonus''' health}} is lost.
* ''Crimson Pact's'' '''bonus''' ability power {{tip|stacks multiplicatively}} with other sources of {{as|% ability power}} but its '''bonus''' health {{tip|stacks additively}} with other sources of {{as|'''bonus''' health}}. With {{iis|Rabadon's Deathcap}} [[Named item effect#Magical Opus|Magical Opus]], the total is ({{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#var:rd}}}}% AP}}|From Rabadon's Deathcap's Magical Opus.}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp1}}*(1+{{#var:rd}}/100)}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Crimson Pact's bonus AP, enhanced by Magical Opus.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' ability power}} and ({{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp2}}*(1+{{#var:rd}}/100)}}}}% AP}}|Crimson Pact's bonus health comes from AP, thus indirectly enhanced by Magical Opus.}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp1}}*{{#var:rd}}*{{#var:cp2}}/10000}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Though Crimson Pact's bonuses do not stack with each other and it's Crimson Pact's bonus AP that is enhanced by Magical Opus, the enhanced part of AP is credited to Rabadon's Deathcap, not Crimson Pact, thus still feeds into Crimson Pact's bonus health.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' health}}.
<!-- ** Case 2: With {{ii|Vigilant Wardstone}} you get a total of ({{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#var:vw1}}}}% AP}}|From Vigilant Wardstone.}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp1}}*(1+{{#var:vw1}}/100)*(1+{{#var:vw2}}/100)}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Crimson Pact's bonus AP comes from bonus health, thus enhanced by both Vigilant Wardstone's effects, multiplicatively.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' ability power}} and ({{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp2}}*(1+{{#var:vw1}}/100)}}}}% AP}}|Crimson Pact's bonus health comes from AP, thus indirectly enhanced by Vigilant Wardstone's AP multiplier, but not bonus HP multiplier (HP stacking is additive).}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:vw2}}+{{#var:cp1}}*{{#var:vw1}}*{{#var:cp2}}*(1+{{#var:vw2}}/100)/10000}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Wardstone's AP multiplier affects Crimsom Pact's AP from bonus HP, previously enhanced by Wardstone's HP multiplier. Resulting additional AP is then converted to HP by Crimson Pact, and added to Wardstone's HP multiplier.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' health}}.
** Case 3: With both {{ii|Rabadon's Deathcap}} and {{ii|Vigilant Wardstone}} you get a total of ({{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:vw1}}+{{#var:rd}}}}}}% AP}}|From Rabadon's Deathcap and Vigilant Wardstone, stacked additively.}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp1}}*(1+({{#var:vw1}}+{{#var:rd}})/100)*(1+{{#var:vw2}}/100)}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Same sequence as Case 2, but with Rabadon's and Wardstone's AP multipliers stacked additively.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' ability power}} and ({{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp2}}*(1+({{#var:vw1}}+{{#var:rd}})/100)}}}}% AP}}|Same sequence as Case 2, but with Rabadon's and Wardstone's AP multipliers stacked additively.}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:vw2}}+{{#var:cp1}}*({{#var:vw1}}+{{#var:rd}})*{{#var:cp2}}*(1+{{#var:vw2}}/100)/10000}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Same sequence as Case 2, but with Rabadon's and Wardstone's AP multipliers stacked additively.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' health}}.
** With {{ii|Cinderhulk}} you get a total of {{tt|{{as|({{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp1}}*(1+{{#var:ch}}/100)}}}}% '''bonus''' health)}}|Crimson Pact's bonus AP comes from bonus health, thus indirectly enhanced by Cinderhulk.}} {{as|'''bonus''' ability power}} and ({{tt|{{as|{{#var:cp2}}% AP}}|From Crimson Pact}} + {{tt|{{as|{{#var:ch}}% '''bonus''' health}}|From Cinderhulk.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' health}}.
<!--** With {{ii|Rabadon's Deathcap}} + {{ii|Cinderhulk}} you get a total of ({{tt|{{as|{{#var:rd}}% AP}}|Same with case 1, from Rabadon's Deathcap.}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:cp1}}*(1+{{#var:rd}}/100)*(1+{{#var:ch}}/100)}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Crimson Pact's bonus AP comes from bonus health, thus enhanced by both Rabadon's Deathcap and Cinderhulk, multiplicatively.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' ability power}} and ({{tt|{{as|{{#expr:{{#var:cp2}}*(1+{{#var:rd}}/100)}}% AP}}|Same with case 1, won't stack with other sources of bonus health (Cinderhulk).}} + {{tt|{{as|{{fd|{{#expr:{{#var:ch}}+{{#var:cp1}}*{{#var:rd}}*{{#var:cp2}}*(1+{{#var:ch}}/100)/10000}}}}% '''bonus''' health}}|Crimson Pact's bonus AP indirectly enhanced by Cinderhulk (refer case 2), then multiplicatively enhanced again by Rabadon's Deathcap. The enhanced part that's credited to Rabadon's Deathcap then enhances Crimson Pact's bonus health (refer case 1). Finally, add the simple bonus health from Cinderhulk.}}) {{as|'''bonus''' health}}.
-->

Q.name: Transfusion
Q.description: Vladimir steals life from the target enemy. When Vladimir's resource is full, Transfusion will benefit from massively increased damage and healing for a brief time.
Q.tooltip:
Vladimir drains his target's lifeforce, dealing {{ basedamagetooltip }} magic damage and restoring {{ basehealtooltip }} Health. After using this Ability twice, Vladimir gains {{ movementspeedonq2 }}% Move Speed for 0.5 seconds and empowers the next use of this Ability for {{ e8 }} seconds.

The empowered version of this Ability deals {{ empowereddamagetooltip }} magic damage instead and restores an additional {{ empoweredhealtooltip }} plus {{ empoweredhealpercenttooltip }} missing Health.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{ci|Vladimir}} drains blood from the target enemy, dealing {{as|magic damage}} and {{tip|heal|healing}} himself. He then generates 1 point of ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' over the {{sti|cooldown}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2: At 2 points of ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'', '''Vladimir''' surges, briefly gaining {{sti|ms|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}} and during which his ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' depletes over a short time.
wiki_detail.blurb3: Casting ''Transfusion'' during the surge consumes all ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' to deal {{ccs|increased damage|magic}} and heal '''Vladimir''' for an additional amount based on {{as|his '''missing''' health}}.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Vladimir''' drains blood from the target enemy, dealing {{as|magic damage}} and {{tip|heal|healing}} himself. He then generates 1 point of ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' over the {{sti|cooldown}}.
wiki_detail.description2: At 2 points of ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'', '''Vladimir''' surges, gaining {{sti|ms|{{as|{{pp|10%;20%;30%;40%|1;6;11;16}} '''bonus''' movement speed}}|ms}} for {{fd|0.5}} seconds while the ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' depletes over {{fd|2.5}} seconds. ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' depletes 75% slower during ''{{ai|Sanguine Pool|Vladimir}}'', ''{{ai|Tides of Blood|Vladimir}}'', or {{tip|stasis (buff)|stasis}}.
wiki_detail.description3: Casting ''Transfusion'' during the surge consumes all ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' to deal {{ccs|85% increased damage|magic}} and {{tip|heal}} '''Vladimir''' for an additional {{pp|30 to 200}} {{as|(+ 5% {{as|(+ 4% per 100 AP)}} of his '''missing''' health)}}. The bonus healing is reduced to{{ft|35%|{{pp|30*0.35 to 200*0.35}} {{as|(+ {{ap|5*0.35}}% {{as|(+ {{ap|4*0.35}}% per 100 AP)}} of his '''missing''' health)}}}}against {{tip|minions}}.
wiki_detail.notes: * Even though the healing effect is visualized as a projectile, the healing itself triggers instantly. * ''Vladimir's'' resource bar indicates his current ''{{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}}'' and changes colors depending on the charge-up stage of his surge. *# White while generating the first stack and while at 1 stack (there is no time-out period). *# Orange while generating the second stack. *# Red while he is surging (''Crimson Rush'' will deplete over {{fd|2.5}} seconds once triggered). ** Each stack generates over-time (''Transfusion's'' cooldown). * '''Vladimir''' can cast {{ai|Sanguine Pool|Vladimir}} and {{ai|Hemoplague|Vladimir}} during ''Transfusion's'' cast time. * The {{tip|Fury|Crimson Rush}} depletes normally while under {{tip|resurrection}} effects.

W.name: Sanguine Pool
W.description: Vladimir sinks into a pool of blood, becoming untargetable for 2 seconds. Additionally, enemies on the pool are slowed and Vladimir siphons life from them.
W.tooltip:
Vladimir sinks into a pool of blood for 2 seconds, gaining {{ hasteboost*100 }}% decaying Move Speed for {{ hasteduration }} second and becoming Untargetable and Ghosted while Slowing enemies in the pool by {{ movespeedmod*-100 }}%.

Vladimir deals {{ totaldamage }} magic damage and restores {{ totalheal }} Health per enemy over the duration.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Vladimir}} sinks into a pool of blood for a short time, becoming {{tip|untargetable}}, {{tip|ghosted}}, and briefly gaining {{as|{{sti|'''bonus''' decaying movement speed}}}}. He cannot use basic attacks and abilities, but can still move.
wiki_detail.blurb2: Enemies within the pool are continually {{tip|slow|slowed}}, and dealt {{as|magic damage}} based on his {{as|bonus health}}. '''Vladimir''' {{tip|heals}} for a portion of the damage dealt.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Vladimir''' sinks into a pool of blood, becoming {{tip|untargetable}} and {{tip|ghosted}} for 2 seconds. He also gains {{sti|ms|{{as|{{fd|37.5}}% '''bonus''' movement speed}}}} that decays exponentially over 1 second.
wiki_detail.description2: Enemies within the pool are dealt {{as|magic damage}} every {{fd|0.5}} seconds over the duration and are {{tip|slow|slowed}} by 40%. '''Vladimir''' {{tip|heals}} himself for 30% of the {{tt|pre-mitigation damage|Damage calculated before modifiers}} dealt, reduced to {{ap|30*0.6}}% against {{tip|minions}}.
wiki_detail.leveling2: {{st|Magic Damage Per Tick|{{ap|20 to 75}} {{as|(+ {{fd|3.75}}% '''bonus''' health)}}|Total Magic Damage|{{ap|20*4 to 75*4}} {{as|(+ 15% '''bonus''' health)}}}}
wiki_detail.description3: '''''Vladimir''' cannot use basic attacks nor abilities during Sanguine Pool, but he can still move. If {{ai|Tides of Blood|Vladimir}} is charging at the time of Sanguine Pool's activation, that ability may still be recast.''
wiki_detail.knockdown:
|notes        = 
* The first tick damages immediately so the final one occurs {{fd|0.5}} seconds before '''Vladimir''' becomes targetable again.
* There is an extra damage tick for 0 damage when '''Vladimir''' becomes targetable again, triggering the same spell effects as the normal ticks (except those which require damage greater than 0 being dealt).
** This will trigger turret aggro onto '''Vladimir''' if an enemy champion is still within the effect radius when ''Sanguine Pool'' ends.
* The {{tip|slow}} ends immediately once affected enemies get out of range.
* '''Vladimir''' can still use [[summoner spell|summoner spells]] and [[Active ability items|item actives]] during ''Sanguine Pool''.
* While unable to attack, '''Vladimir''' can still input attack commands - causing him to follow his attack target. 
** '''Vladimir's''' {{stil|attack range}} is reduced{{ft|to 0|by 450}}while pooled - causing him to attempt to move right up to his attack target.

E.name: Tides of Blood
E.description: Vladimir pays his own health to charge up a reservoir of blood which, when released, deals damage in the area around him but can be blocked by enemy units.
E.tooltip:
Begin Charging: Vladimir charges up a reservoir of blood, spending up to {{ chargehealthtooltip }} Health. While at full charge, Vladimir is Slowed by 20%.

Release: Vladimir unleashes a torrent of blood missiles at surrounding enemies, dealing between {{ mindamagetooltip }} and {{ maxdamagetooltip }} magic damage based on charge time. If this Ability was charged for at least 1 second, it also Slows targets by {{ slowpercent }}% for 0.5 seconds.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}
E.resource: Channeling Cost {{ e2 }}% of max Health ({{ chargehealthtooltip }})

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Vladimir}} {{tip|channel|charges}} up to a brief moment to ramp up a {{as|health cost}}. ''Tides of Blood'' can be recast within the duration, and does so automatically afterwards or if {{tip|interrupt|interrupted}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2: {{sbc|Recast:}} '''Vladimir''' unleashes a nova of blood bolts that each deal {{as|magic damage}} to the first enemy hit based on {{as|his '''maximum''' health}}, which is further increased based on charge time. If he charged long enough, enemies hit are also briefly {{tip|slow|slowed}}.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Vladimir''' {{tip|channel|charges}} for up to {{fd|1.5}} seconds, during which he increases ''Tides of Blood's'' damage over the first second of the channel, and becomes {{tip|slow|slowed}} by 20% afterwards for the remaining duration. ''Tides of Blood'' can be recast within the duration, and does so automatically afterwards or if it is {{tip|interrupt|interrupted}}.
wiki_detail.description2: {{sbc|Recast:}} '''Vladimir''' unleashes a nova of 15 blood bolts around himself that each deal {{as|magic damage}} to the first enemy hit, increased based on charge time up to the first second.
wiki_detail.leveling2: {{st|Minimum Magic Damage|{{ap|30 to 90}} {{as|(+ {{fd|1.5}}% '''maximum''' health)}} {{as|(+ 35% AP)}}|Maximum Magic Damage|{{ap|60 to 180}} {{as|(+ 6% '''maximum''' health)}} {{as|(+ 80% AP)}}}}
wiki_detail.description3: If ''Tides of Blood'' was charged for at least 1 second, enemies hit are also {{tip|slow|slowed}} for {{fd|0.5}} seconds.
wiki_detail.description4: ''Enemies can intercept multiple bolts, but can be damaged only once.''
wiki_detail.description5: ''If '''Vladimir''' is below {{as|12% of his '''maximum''' health}}, Tides of Blood will not cost any {{as|health}}.''
wiki_detail.notes:
* The spell indicator shows 11 equally spaced missile indicators when hovering the ability in the HUD, however the spell actually casts 15 equally spaced missiles.
* The {{as|health}} cost may still drop '''Vladimir''' below the specified amount if he is above it. This is verified for every tick of {{as|health}} cost, i.e if the first tick drops him below it, the next ones will stop affecting him if he remains below it.
* The following table refers for interactions while '''Vladimir''' is {{tip|channel|channeling}}:
{{ct|charge|cast={{ai|Sanguine Pool|Vladimir}} is usable. {{ai|Transfusion|Vladimir}} and {{ai|Hemoplague|Vladimir}} both interrupt after {{fd|0.25}} seconds.|items=interrupts,true,interrupts,false,interrupts|spells=true,true,interrupts,interrupts,interrupts}}

R.name: Hemoplague
R.description: Vladimir infects an area with a virulent plague. Affected enemies take increased damage for the duration. After a few seconds, Hemoplague deals magic damage to infected enemies and heals Vladimir for each enemy Champion hit.
R.tooltip: Vladimir creates a virulent plague, causing its victims to take {{ e2 }}% increased damage from all sources for {{ e4 }} seconds. After it expires, Vladimir deals {{ damage }} magic damage to all infected targets. Vladimir restores {{ damage }} Health if he hits a champion, and restores an additional {{ secondaryhealingtooltip }} Health for each champion beyond the first.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Vladimir}} unleashes a plague at the target location that infects nearby enemies hit for a few seconds, increasing the damage they take from all sources.
wiki_detail.blurb2: After the duration, infected targets are dealt {{as|magic damage}}, which heals '''Vladimir''' for each {{tip|champion}} hit.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Vladimir''' spreads a virulent plague at the target location that infects enemies hit for 4 seconds, increasing the damage they take from all sources by 10%.
wiki_detail.description2: After the duration, the infection bursts to deal {{as|magic damage}} to all affected targets and, after a {{fd|0.4}}-second delay, {{tip|heal}} '''Vladimir''' for each infected champion, reduced by 40% for champions beyond the first.
wiki_detail.notes:
* ''Hemoplague'' will amplify almost all sources and types of damage (including damage from neutral monsters but excluding {{tip|true damage|{{as|true damage}}}}, with percent modifiers stacking multiplicatively).
** ''Hemoplague'' amplifies itself for an actual {{ap|150*1.1 to 350*1.1}} {{as|(+ {{fd|77}}% AP)}} damage.
* ''Hemoplague's'' modifier to incoming damage stacks additively with {{cai|Unbreakable Will|Alistar}} for a total damage reduction of 45/55/65%.{{tt|*|this might be unintended}}
* {{tip|Spell shield}} does not negate the detonation.
